perturbation.original_text: ARTICLE 16 - TAXES 16.1 The Company will ensure timely remittance of all tax liabilities and ensure specific adherence to any specific tax considerations. HGF will ensure maximum tax reduction and where possible elimination of any tax consideration.
perturbation.changed_text: ARTICLE 16 - TAXES 16.1 The Company will use reasonable efforts to remit tax liabilities in a timely manner and consider relevant tax considerations. HGF will attempt to maximize tax reduction and possibly eliminate tax considerations.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text weakens the obligation regarding tax compliance. Replacing "ensure timely remittance" with "use reasonable efforts to remit" introduces ambiguity about the standard of compliance. Similarly, "ensure specific adherence" becomes "consider relevant tax considerations," lowering the required level of commitment. Finally, changing 'ensure maximum tax reduction and where possible elimination' to 'attempt to maximize tax reduction and possibly eliminate' is vague. This could lead to non-compliance with tax laws.
perturbation.law_citation: 26 U.S. Code § 6672 - Failure to collect and pay over tax, or attempt to evade or defeat tax
perturbation.law_explanation: The modification contradicts 26 U.S. Code § 6672 because it suggests a lower standard of tax compliance than what is legally required. The IRS can impose penalties on individuals responsible for willfully failing to collect or pay over taxes. The original text implies strict adherence, while the modified text allows for subjective interpretation of 'reasonable efforts', and introduces a possibility for non-compliance with federal tax obligations.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: 26 U.S. Code § 6672 - Failure to collect and pay over tax, or attempt to evade or defeat tax | U.S. Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Quick search by citation: Title Section Go! 26 U.S. Code § 6672 - Failure to collect and pay over tax, or attempt to evade or defeat tax U.S. Code Notes Authorities (CFR) prev | next (a) General rule Any person required to collect, truthfully account for, and pay over any tax imposed by this title who willfully fails to collect such tax , or truthfully account for and pay over such tax , or willfully attempts in any manner to evade or defeat any such tax or the payment thereof, shall, in addition to other penalties provided by law, be liable to a penalty equal to the total amount of the tax evaded, or not collected, or not accounted for and paid over. No penalty shall be imposed under section 6653 or part II of subchapter A of chapter 68 for any offense to which this section is applicable. (b) Preliminary notice requirement (1) In general No penalty shall be imposed under subsection (a) unless the Secretary notifies the taxpayer in writing by mail to an address as determined under section 6212(b) or in person that the taxpayer shall be subject to an assessment of such penalty. (2) Timing of notice The mailing of the notice described in paragraph (1) (or, in the case of such a notice delivered in person , such delivery) shall precede any notice and demand of any penalty under subsection (a) by at least 60 days. (3) Statute of limitations If a notice described in paragraph (1) with respect to any penalty is mailed or delivered in person before the expiration of the period provided by section 6501 for the assessment of such penalty (determined without regard to this paragraph), the period provided by such section for the assessment of such penalty shall not expire before the later of— (A) the date 90 days after the date on which such notice was mailed or delivered in person , or (B) if there i

Failure to collect and pay over tax, or attempt to evade or defeat tax (a) General rule Any person required to collect, truthfully account for, and pay over any tax imposed by this title who willfully
perturbation.justification: The modified contract introduces ambiguity regarding tax compliance, which contradicts 26 U.S. Code § 6672. The original text ensures 'timely remittance' and 'specific adherence,' but the changed text uses terms like 'reasonable efforts' and 'consider relevant tax considerations,' weakening the commitment. The scraped law snippet 1 states that "Any person required to collect, truthfully account for, and pay over any tax imposed by this title who willfully fails to collect such tax , or truthfully account for and pay over such tax , or willfully attempts in any manner to evade or defeat any such tax or the payment thereof, shall, in addition to other penalties provided by law, be liable to a penalty." This indicates a strict obligation. The modification introduces a lower standard, suggesting that failure to comply fully may be acceptable if 'reasonable efforts' were made, which could be interpreted as attempting to 'evade or defeat tax', thus contradicting the law.

perturbation.original_text: 9.1 To maintain all financial records of the Company and provide quarterly and annual reporting to all Company stakeholders. All records are kept under US GAAP compliance standards.
perturbation.changed_text: 9.1 To maintain the Company's financial records and provide periodic reporting to Company stakeholders. Records are kept under accounting standards.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "maintain all financial records" to "maintain financial records" creates ambiguity. The removal of "quarterly and annual" weakens the reporting frequency requirements. Removing the US GAAP reference means the financial reports are subject to flexible interpretation that is not a standard.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Securities Exchange Act of 1934 - Reporting Requirements
perturbation.law_explanation: The modification contradicts the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 (15 U.S.C. § 78m), which mandates specific and standardized reporting requirements for publicly traded companies. NVOS, being a US reporting publicly traded corporation (as mentioned in Article 9.5), is subject to these requirements. By removing the specificity of 'quarterly and annual' reporting and 'US GAAP compliance standards,' the modified text fails to ensure compliance with the regulations enforced by the SEC, potentially leading to inaccurate or incomplete financial disclosures.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: 15 U.S. Code § 78m - Periodical and other reports | U.S. Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Quick search by citation: Title Section Go! 15 U.S. Code § 78m - Periodical and other reports U.S. Code Notes Authorities (CFR) prev | next (a) Reports by issuer of security; contents Every issuer of a security registered pursuant to section 78 l of this title shall file with the Commission, in accordance with such rules and regulations as the Commission may prescribe as necessary or appropriate for the proper protection of investors and to insure fair dealing in the security— (1) such information and documents (and such copies thereof) as the Commission shall require to keep reasonably current the information and documents required to be included in or filed with an application or registration statement filed pursuant to section 78l of this title , except that the Commission may not require the filing of any material contract wholly executed before July 1, 1962 . (2) such annual reports (and such copies thereof), certified if required by the rules and regulations of the Commission by independent public accountants, and such quarterly reports (and such copies thereof), as the Commission may prescribe. Every issuer of a security registered on a national securities exchange shall also file a duplicate original of such information, documents, and reports with the exchange . Periodical and other reports (a) Reports by issuer of security; contents Every issuer of a security registered pursuant to section 78l of this title shall file with the Commission, in accordance with such rules and regulations as the Commission may prescribe as necessary or appropriate for the proper protection of investors and to insure fair dealing in the security- (1) such information and documents (and suc
perturbation.justification: The modified text creates ambiguity and weakens legally required reporting standards for publicly traded companies. The original text mandated 'quarterly and annual reporting' according to 'US GAAP compliance standards.' The changed text replaces this with 'periodic reporting' under 'accounting standards,' which contradicts the requirements outlined in 15 U.S.C. § 78m. This section mandates that issuers of registered securities must file 'annual reports' and 'quarterly reports' with the Commission, as seen in both scraped law snippets. By removing the specific frequency and standard, the modified text fails to ensure compliance with SEC regulations, creating a significant risk of non-compliance and inaccurate financial disclosures.

perturbation.original_text: 10.2 To maintain positive relations with agencies (government and environmental) ensuring continuing land use and development.
perturbation.changed_text: 10.2 To attempt to maintain cordial relations with agencies (government and environmental) to support land use and development.
perturbation.explanation: The changed text uses "attempt to maintain cordial relations" instead of "maintain positive relations," introducing ambiguity. "Ensuring continuing land use and development" is changed to "to support land use and development", weakening the assurance of compliance with environmental regulations.
perturbation.law_citation: 33 U.S. Code § 1342 - National pollutant discharge elimination system
perturbation.law_explanation: The modification contradicts the Clean Water Act (33 U.S.C. § 1342) requirements, specifically regarding NPDES permits. The original text implies a proactive role in ensuring compliance with regulations pertaining to land use and development. The changed version, by suggesting only an 'attempt' to maintain relations and merely 'supporting' land use, undermines the obligation to strictly adhere to environmental laws and permit requirements. Agricultural activities are regulated by this Act; therefore this lack of assurance creates potential violations.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: 33 U.S. Code § 1342 - National pollutant discharge elimination system | U.S. Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Quick search by citation: Title Section Go! 33 U.S. Code § 1342 - National pollutant discharge elimination system U.S. Code Notes Authorities (CFR) prev | next (a) Permits for discharge of pollutants (1) Except as provided in sections 1328 and 1344 of this title, the Administrator may, after opportunity for public hearing issue a permit for the discharge of any pollutant, or combination of pollutants, notwithstanding section 1311(a) of this title , upon condition that such discharge will meet either (A) all applicable requirements under sections 1311, 1312, 1316, 1317, 1318, and 1343 of this title, or (B) prior to the taking of necessary implementing actions relating to all such requirements, such conditions as the Administrator determines are necessary to carry out the provisions of this chapter. (2) The Administrator shall prescribe conditions for such permits to assure compliance with the requirements of paragraph (1) of this subsection, including conditions on data and information collection, reporting, and such other requirements as he deems appropriate. (3) The permit program of the Administrator under paragraph (1) of this subsection, and permits issued thereunder, shall be subject to the same terms, conditions, and requirements as apply to a State permit program and permits issued thereunder under subsection (b) of this section. (4) All permits for discharges into the navigable waters issued pursuant to section 407 of this title shall be deemed to be permits issued under this subchapter, and permits issued under this subchapter shall be deemed to be permits issued under section 407 of this title , and shall continue in force and effect for their term unless revoked, modified, or suspended in accordance with the provisions of this chapter. National pollutant discharge elimination system (a) Permits for discharge of pollutants (1) Except as provided in sections 1328 and 1344 of this title , the Administrator may, after opportunity for public hearing issue a permit for the discharge of any pollutant, or combination of pollutants, notwithstanding section 1311(a) of this title , upon condition that suc
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'maintain positive relations' with 'attempt to maintain cordial relations' and 'ensuring continuing land use and development' with 'to support land use and development'. This introduces ambiguity and weakens the assurance of compliance with environmental regulations, potentially violating the Clean Water Act, specifically regarding NPDES permits (33 U.S.C. § 1342), as it undermines the obligation to strictly adhere to environmental laws. The original text suggests a proactive role, whereas the modified text suggests a more passive role. Snippet 1 and 2 both mention 33 U.S. Code § 1342 which pertains to the National Pollutant Discharge Elimination System and the requirements for permits related to the discharge of pollutants, highlighting the importance of maintaining compliance with environmental standards which the changed text undermines.
